title: DOC_THINK observes memories via a bounded (created_at,id) keyset query, not a whole-table load every cycle
behavior: GIVEN the documents domain's hourly observe cycle WHEN it gathers unprocessed memories THEN it calls data_layer.memories.load_after_cursor(cursor_id, limit) which returns AT MOST `limit` rows (never the whole table), ascending (created_at, id); limit = _memories_per_cycle() * 3 (a 2x scan margin over the per-cycle useful cap so noise-filtering still yields ~per_cycle useful). The SELECT lists ONLY the non-embedding _ROW_COLUMNS (never the embedding vector) — the OOM guard. cursor_id and limit are psycopg2 bind params (no string interpolation).

GIVEN a last_processed cursor id that EXISTS WHEN load_after_cursor runs THEN it returns only rows strictly after the cursor by the composite keyset (created_at, id) > the cursor row's (created_at, id) (resolved by an id-lookup of the cursor's created_at) ORDER BY created_at, id ASC LIMIT limit — same-instant memories (equal created_at) are ordered by id, none skipped or duplicated.

GIVEN NO cursor yet (first run) WHEN load_after_cursor runs THEN it returns the OLDEST `limit` rows ascending; the backlog is drained across successive hourly cycles (accelerated by catchup mode), never loaded whole in one cycle.

GIVEN a cursor id NOT found (memory deleted) WHEN load_after_cursor runs THEN it falls back to the most-recent `limit` rows (reversed to ascending), mirroring the prior all_memories[-per_cycle:] — EXPLICITLY this jumps the cursor forward to the recent window and does not revisit the older backlog between the deleted cursor and that window (parity with today).

GIVEN the query WHEN it executes THEN it is backed by a new index memories(created_at, id) (migration 002_memories_created_at_id_index.sql: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS, no BEGIN/COMMIT — the migrator wraps each file in one transaction and runs on start) so it is a bounded index scan, not a full-table sort.

GIVEN catchup-mode detection WHEN _observe returns its context THEN total_memory_count and total_unprocessed_before_filter are computed from bounded COUNT queries — total via count_memories() (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM memories, a plain total), remaining via count_after_cursor(cursor) (SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE (created_at,id) > cursor, index-backed) — NOT from len(batch). First run reports the full table count as remaining (so catchup fires on a big fresh install); a deleted cursor reports the loaded recent-window size. This preserves the existing behavior: total_unprocessed_before_filter > 500 -> CATCHUP_INTERVAL_SECONDS (10-min cycles), the 'Catchup: N total remaining' focus, the '(N of M total)' prompt header, and the metrics snapshot stay honest at scale.

GIVEN _observe's existing noise-filter and cursor-advance logic WHEN it runs over the bounded batch THEN behavior is unchanged (noise pre-filtered, up to _memories_per_cycle() useful collected, cursor advanced to raw_last_id = the last EXAMINED row incl. trailing noise). FORWARD PROGRESS: because the cursor advances past examined noise each cycle, a bounded window that is entirely noise still advances by up to `limit` rows next cycle — no permanent stall.

OUT OF SCOPE: the all_doc_summaries block (bounded already), load_all() itself (retained; note it becomes unused by _observe), and noise-filter logic. DEPLOY NOTE: at switchover the new (created_at,id) order may skip or re-offer a few rows sharing the existing cursor's exact created_at, once (bounded).
implements: data_layer/memories.py::load_after_cursor (bounded (created_at,id) keyset, _ROW_COLUMNS only, first-run + cursor-not-found fallbacks, bind params)
data_layer/memories.py::count_after_cursor (index-backed COUNT after the keyset cursor; None when the cursor was deleted so the caller falls back to the window size)
migrations/002_memories_created_at_id_index.sql (CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_memories_created_at_id ON memories (created_at, id))

apps/documents/domain.py::_observe (use load_after_cursor(last_processed_id, per_cycle*3); recompute total_memory_count via count_memories(), total_unprocessed_before_filter via count_after_cursor; keep noise-filter + cursor-advance)

tests.rubric: load_after_cursor: (a) no cursor -> oldest `limit` ascending, never > limit. (b) cursor present -> only rows strictly after (created_at,id), ascending, <= limit. (c) TIE-SAFETY: the keyset compares the (created_at,id) tuple, not created_at alone. (d) cursor NOT found -> most-recent `limit` reversed to ascending (fallback), not empty. (e) BOUNDED: the LIMIT param IS the limit. (f) NO-EMBEDDING: every query lists _ROW_COLUMNS, never 'embedding' (also extended in test_memories_no_embedding_fetch.py). (g) BIND PARAMS: cursor_id/limit passed as params, query string has no interpolation. Plus count_after_cursor: empty->total, found->COUNT keyset, deleted->None.

_observe context: (1) cursor far from end + large table -> total_memory_count == count_memories() and total_unprocessed_before_filter == count_after_cursor (NOT len(batch); > 500 keeps catchup honest). (2) first run -> total_unprocessed_before_filter == full table count (catchup fires on a big fresh install). (3) deleted cursor -> total_unprocessed_before_filter == the recent window size loaded. (4) FORWARD PROGRESS: an all-noise bounded window still advances raw_last_id to the last examined row.

On the test host seed memories beyond `limit`; assert load_after_cursor returns <= limit (not the whole table), EXPLAIN shows an index scan on idx_memories_created_at_id (no Seq Scan + full Sort), and one _observe cycle no longer materializes the whole table while total_unprocessed_before_filter still reflects the true remaining (catchup honest). Capture fenced text.

notes: ev-103 (operator-authored perf/OOM follow-up). Root cause: memories.load_all() (no LIMIT) was called by _observe every hourly cycle -> whole-table materialization (~119,637 rows / ~120MB in prod) plus a Python linear scan for the cursor; no memories(created_at) index, so the ORDER BY full-sorts. Fix: bounded (created_at,id) keyset query + supporting index + switch the single caller. Correctness preserved (noise-filter + cursor-advance unchanged); first-run and cursor-not-found fallbacks retained. Distinct from the earlier no-embedding fix. Operator forks (approved): A composite (created_at,id) keyset + index; 2x per_cycle scan margin; oldest-first + catchup (newest-first backfill deferred as a separate UX item). Operator pins honored: catchup counters recomputed from COUNT queries (not len-derived); first-run WILL exercise the catchup path (fresh install). Large existing backlog drains over ~days (one-time, non-blocking; faster under catchup 10-min cycles).
